title: Dredge Operators
description: Operate dredge to remove sand, gravel, or other materials in order to excavate and maintain navigable channels in waterways.

tasks.text: Move levers to position dredges for excavation, to engage hydraulic pumps, to raise and lower suction booms, and to control rotation of cutterheads.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start and stop engines to operate equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start power winches that draw in or let out cables to change positions of dredges, or pull in and let out cables manually.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pump water to clear machinery pipelines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lower anchor poles to verify depths of excavations, using winches, or scan depth gauges to determine depths of excavations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct or assist workers placing shore anchors and cables, laying additional pipes from dredges to shore, and pumping water from pontoons.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
